pr.title: Improvements to split up: Dolphin support
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Support-directory slice split from PR #2617, focused on Dolphin THP decoder code and sysdolphin baselib matching cleanups. The merged diff mainly refactored THPDec MCU row decompression to use a local THPFileInfo pointer consistently and a typed THPFileInfoMCUBufferView cast instead of a raw offset-derived THPCoeff**. Smaller baselib changes adjusted statement ordering in hsd_3B34.c, simplified a synth sentinel comparison to the direct signed constant -0x10001, and removed a blank line in cobj.c after review rejected a PAD_STACK-based attempt as not a real improvement. Review also pushed back on an inaccurate particle.c string-label/table attempt, which does not appear in the final changed files.
what_changed: In extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c, __THPDecompressiMCURowNxN now stores __THPInfo in a local THPFileInfo* info and uses info->xPixelSize, info->components, and info->quantTabs rather than repeatedly referencing __THPInfo.

The THP MCU buffer access in __THPDecompressiMCURowNxN changed from a local raw pointer cast, THPCoeff** mcuBuffer = (THPCoeff**)((u8*)info + 0x10), to repeated typed casts through THPFileInfoMCUBufferView and its mcuBuffer field.
The THP decode and inverse-DCT calls now pass ((THPFileInfoMCUBufferView*) info)->mcuBuffer[N] directly for Y, U, and V components.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34.c, assignments to jmp_buf and temp_r4 were moved after reads from lbl_804DEB88, apparently to improve matching-sensitive instruction ordering.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c, a sentinel check changed from (u32)(x8 + 0x10000) == -1U to the simpler direct comparison x8 == -0x10001.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.c, the final merged diff only removes a blank line in HSD_CObjSetUpVector; a reviewed PAD_STACK(4) attempt was rejected and is not present in the final diff.

Review excerpts show a particle.c change attempted to replace direct string literals with a large byte-array label, but reviewers called it inaccurate/regressive and particle.c is absent from the final changed_files list, implying that work was dropped from the merged slice.
smart_moves: Splitting the larger matching-improvement branch by support directory made the changes easier to review and helped isolate Dolphin/support code from unrelated work.
Using a typed THPFileInfoMCUBufferView view communicates the structure overlay being relied on better than a magic +0x10 raw pointer cast.
Caching __THPInfo in a local info pointer and consistently reusing it reduces repeated global references while preserving the same THP decoder logic.
The synth comparison rewrite expresses the intended sentinel value directly, which is clearer than relying on unsigned wraparound arithmetic.
Reviewer pushback was incorporated by avoiding questionable particle string-label work and avoiding a PAD_STACK-only cobj change that did not address deeper function issues.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK overlays or partially known structs, prefer introducing a named view type for a known field layout over burying offsets in raw casts, provided the layout evidence is strong.
Small source-order changes can matter for matching; moving loads or assignments may be a valid tactic when it reflects codegen order rather than cosmetic preference.
Do not turn obvious direct string literals into large anonymous byte arrays just to force data layout unless there is evidence the original code used a table or packed blob.
A stack-padding macro is not automatically an improvement; if a function has fundamental issues, reviewers may reject PAD_STACK churn as masking rather than solving the mismatch.
When slicing a large PR, make sure the final file list reflects what actually merged; planned-path files such as particle.c, sislib.c, or other baselib files may have been dropped during review.
naming_conventions: Typed view name observed: THPFileInfoMCUBufferView for accessing mcuBuffer within THPFileInfo storage.
Existing sysdolphin/baselib names retained: HSD_CObjSetUpVector, HSD_CObjGetUpVector, hsd_803B46D4, HSD_Synth_8038ADD0.
Temporary/generated labels remain in use where symbols are still unknown, including lbl_804DEB88 and lbl_804C4540.
Reviewer feedback discourages replacing readable string literals with opaque labels like lbl_8040A540 when that is likely inaccurate to original developer source.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace repeated global __THPInfo accesses with a local pointer when that produces better register allocation or load ordering.
Use a typed struct/view cast to access data at a known offset instead of introducing a local raw pointer that may alter codegen.
Reorder local assignments around memory reads in hsd_3B34.c to match the target instruction schedule.
Simplify unsigned wraparound sentinel checks into signed literal comparisons when that produces the desired match and is semantically clearer.
Avoid PAD_STACK as a superficial matching tactic when the surrounding function has unresolved structural/codegen problems.

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.feedback: The large byte-array label for MCC/HIO error strings was most likely inaccurate to what the original developers did. Suggested trying a const char* table if it matched, otherwise reverting because direct string literals were more likely.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Changing a case from the direct string "MCC is no initialize" to lbl_8040A540 caused a regression due to not using the string.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Adding PAD_STACK(4) in HSD_CObjSetUpVector was not considered an improvement because the function has other fundamental issues.

follow_up_queries: Inspect the definition of THPFileInfoMCUBufferView and confirm whether the mcuBuffer offset/layout is documented well enough for future THP work.
Revisit HSD_CObjSetUpVector for the fundamental issues mentioned by PsiLupan rather than relying on PAD_STACK.
For particle.c MCC/HIO error strings, test direct literals versus a const char* table before introducing packed byte-array labels.
Compare this support-directory slice against the source PR #2617 to identify which planned files were intentionally dropped before merge.
Check decomp-dev CI results for the final slice to quantify the exact matched-code/data effect after review changes.
